g.text: This is the direct way for the purification of beings, for the overcoming of sorrow and lamentation,
for the subsiding of discomfort and distress, for the attainment of the right path, for the realization of Nibbāna
ekāyano ayaṁ bhikkhave maggo sattānaṁ visuddhiyā … nibbānassa sacchikiriyāya
FOUR ESTABLISHMENTS OF MINDFULNESS · cattāro satipaṭṭhānā
With continuous effort, clearly aware and being present, having removed craving and distress with regard to the world
ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ
Dwells observing the body in and of itself
Breathes mindfully; discerns long and short breaths;
trains to experience the whole body
Is aware of posture while walking, standing,
sitting, or lying down. In whatever way the body
is oriented, he discerns it accordingly.
Acts with clear awareness in all activities: going,
returning, looking, bending, eating, dressing, drinking,
defecating, urinating, speaking, and keeping silent.
Reviews the body from the soles upwards and hair
downwards as full of various impurities: skin, flesh, sinews,
bones, organs, stomach contents, fat, grease, and fluids.
Simile: bag with two openings full of many sorts of grains
Reviewing the elements in the body
in terms of the four elements:
like a butcher dividing a cow at a crossroads.
‘This body too is of the same nature;’
Dwells observing the felt experience in and of itself
Dwells observing the mind in and of itself
Discerns as it actually is — yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti

text.tspan: · yathā ca anuppannānaṁ nīvaraṇānaṁ uppādo hoti
④ how arisen hindrance is abandoned
· yathā ca uppannānaṁ nīvaraṇānaṁ pahānaṁ hoti
⑤ how abandoned hindrance does not arise again
· yathā ca pahīnassa nīvaraṇassa āyatiṁ anuppādo hoti

g.text: 4b. FIVE AGGREGATES SUBJECT TO CLINGING · pañcupādānakkhandhā
Discerns with regard to the aggregate:
4c. SIX INTERNAL AND EXTERNAL SENSE BASES · saḷāyatana
Dwells discerning the internal and external sense base and:

text.tspan: ① the fetter that arises dependent on both
③ how arisen fetter is abandoned
④ how it does not arise again
Discerns when an Awakening Factor (A.F.) is:
· yathā ca anuppannassa bojjhaṅgassa uppādo hoti
④ how arisen A.F. reaches fulfillment through cultivation
· yathā ca uppannassa bojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya pāripūrī hoti

g.text: Dwells observing the mental qualities in and of themselves with regard to the Four Noble Truths
Discerns as it actually is — yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti
This is the arising of suffering
This is the end of suffering
The way to the end of suffering
without clinging to anything in the world · na ca kiñci loke upādiyati
Observing the arising nature, vanishing nature, or both
With mindfulness established, dwells independent, and without clinging
sati paccupaṭṭhitā · anissito ca viharati · na ca kiñci loke upādiyati
Whoever develops and cultivates these four satipaṭṭhāna,
one of two fruits may be expected, in as little as seven days ... months ... years:
"This is the direct way, bhikkhus, for…"
